title: War Memorial & Museum
description: The Punjab State War Heroes' Memorial and Museum in Amritsar showcases the brave deeds and military history of Punjab from ancient times to the modern era. Features a massive 45-meter-high stainless steel sword monument, fighter jets, tanks, and galleries depicting battle history.
address: Amritsar-Attari Road, Near Bus Stand, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Houses the tallest stainless steel sword monument in the world at 45 meters
Displays decommissioned fighter jets, tanks, and military artillery
Galleries cover military history from the Vedic period to the Kargil War
Memorial Wall inscribed with the names of over 5,000 war heroes from Punjab
Opened in 2019, dedicated to Punjab's soldiers who served in Indian armed forces

title: Company Bagh Summer Palace
description: The summer retreat retreat built by Maharaja Ranjit Singh inside the Ram Bagh Gardens (commonly known as Company Bagh). It exhibits heritage Sikh architecture, beautiful woodwork, weapons, coins, and paintings from the reign of the Lion of Punjab.
address: Ram Bagh Garden, Mall Road, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Summer palace of the great Sikh emperor Maharaja Ranjit Singh
Displays historic weapons including swords, shields, and muskets from the Sikh Empire
Contains rare coins from the Sikh, Mughal, and British periods
Oil paintings depict major battles and court scenes of the Sikh Empire era
The surrounding Ram Bagh Gardens are modeled after the Shalimar Gardens of Lahore

title: Partition Museum
description: The world's first museum dedicated to the 1947 partition of British India. Located in the grand Town Hall building, it documents the division of Punjab and Bengal through oral histories, letters, photographs, and personal memorabilia donated by survivors.
address: Town Hall, Shastri Market, Hall Gate, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: The world's first museum dedicated entirely to the 1947 Partition of India
Housed in the restored heritage Town Hall building at Hall Gate
Contains personal artifacts donated by partition survivors — letters, clothes, utensils
Oral history recordings of survivors are available for visitors to listen to
Seven themed galleries chronicle the events leading up to and following the partition

title: Maharaja Ranjit Singh Panorama
description: A unique multimedia museum in Ram Bagh that presents the life and battles of Maharaja Ranjit Singh in a grand circular format. Features a stunning 360-degree panoramic painting, battle sound effects, and detailed three-dimensional historical displays.
address: Ram Bagh Garden, Mall Road, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Features a massive 360-degree circular painting depicting Maharaja Ranjit Singh's life
Life-sized three-dimensional dioramas recreate key battles of the Sikh Empire
Immersive battle sound effects transport visitors into the era of the Lion of Punjab
Chronicles the rise, reign, and legacy of Maharaja Ranjit Singh (1780–1839)
Located adjacent to the Company Bagh Summer Palace in Ram Bagh Garden

title: Digital Sikh Museum
description: Located underground in the Golden Temple Entrance Plaza, this museum uses high-tech digital projection, mapping displays, and documentaries to present the spiritual history of the Gurus and the Golden Temple itself.
address: Golden Temple Entrance Plaza (Underground), Amritsar, Punjab 143006
bulletPoints: Located in an underground gallery beneath the Golden Temple entrance plaza
Uses cutting-edge digital projection and mapping to tell the story of Sikhism
Interactive touchscreens let visitors explore the lives of the ten Sikh Gurus
Features a documentary theater with short films on Sikh history and philosophy
Completely free to visit and air-conditioned throughout — a welcome respite
